title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — Americas — Q2 FY2026 (May – July 2026)
summary: The Americas enters the most significant crypto regulatory implementation phase since 2013 BSA guidance. United States federal stablecoin framework (GENIUS Act) takes effect July 1 with three-agency rulemaking in progress, while FinCEN's fundamental BSA/AML reform comment period closes June 9—both reshaping compliance infrastructure requirements for every VASP, bank, and stablecoin issuer. title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — EMEA — Q2 FY2026 (May – July 2026)
summary: European regulators are shifting from policy design to active enforcement across 27 member states simultaneously. The EU's MiCA regime becomes fully operational July 1 with over 30 technical standards taking effect, while AMLA launches direct supervision of high-risk CASPs in Q3. UK's FCA opens its authorization gateway September 2026, creating parallel compliance deadlines for firms operating across both jurisdictions. title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — Asia-Pacific — Q2 FY2026 (May – July 2026)
summary: Asia-Pacific enters the most synchronized compliance infrastructure activation in the region's history. Fourteen jurisdictions face simultaneous VASP licensing deadlines, institutional custody launches, and AML enforcement escalations through September 2026. Japan's reclassification of crypto as investment products and Vietnam's 0.1% transaction tax create immediate compliance infrastructure demand across exchanges, banks, and traditional financial institutions entering crypto markets.

title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — NAM — Q2 FY2026 (May – July 2026)
summary: International standard-setters FATF, FSB, and IOSCO are converging on Q3-Q4 2026 deliverables for DeFi AML, stablecoin oversight, and crypto market conduct—frameworks that will cascade into U.S. and Canadian rulemaking through 2027. NAM Account Executives should position now with FIs, exchanges, and regulators who need compliance infrastructure *before* domestic rules codify these global standards.

title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — Global — Q2 FY2026 (May – July 2026)
summary: International standard-setters are synchronizing Q3-Q4 2026 guidance releases across AML/CFT for DeFi (FATF), stablecoin oversight (FSB), and market conduct frameworks (IOSCO)—creating a 90-day window where multinational exchanges, banks, and regulators need compliance infrastructure before hard national implementation deadlines hit in 2027. This is the last quarter to position regulatory intelligence and baseline compliance tooling before budget freezes.
